body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Non-fabrication — spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, DMN, creative insight.

**Mind-wandering in Parkinson'sdisease hallucinations reflects primary visual and default network coupling.**. Cortex.. PMID: 32058090. DOI: 10.1016/j.cortex.2019.12.023. Visual hallucinations are an underappreciated symptom affecting the majority of patients during the natural history of Parkinson'sdisease. Little is known about other forms of abstract and internally generated cognition - such as mind-wandering - in this population, but emerging evidence suggests that an interplay between the brain'sprimary visual and default networks might play acrucial role in both internally generated imagery and hallucinations.
